div.p: Why the kernel stack pointer must change when switching processes

g.text: What Happens if TSS.ESP0 is NOT Updated
Correct: ESP0 → current process kernel stack
Bug: Stale ESP0 → wrong stack → corruption

div.p: Result: When Process A resumes, its saved registers are corrupted. Process B's syscall clobbered Process A's kernel stack. This causes random crashes, silent data corruption, or triple faults.

text.tspan: // 1. Save old process state to its PCB
// 2. CRITICAL: Update TSS.ESP0 before restoring new process
// This ensures ring transitions use correct kernel stack
// 4. Restore new process state and resume
// 1. Save old process state to its PCB
// 2. CRITICAL: Update TSS.ESP0 before restoring new process
// This ensures ring transitions use correct kernel stack
// 4. Restore new process state and resume

div.p: Saved EIP, CS, EFLAGS Saved EAX, EBX, ECX... [growing down]
Saved context from last switch [frozen state]
[FROZEN] Full register state saved for resume
